LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER MARGARET MURRAY'S PETITION OF MANDATE. THE COURT MAKES DISCLOSURES REGARDING PAST PROFESSIONAL RELATIONSHIP WITH BOTH COUNSEL PRESENT AS REFLECTED IN THE TRANSCRIPT OF THE CERTIFIEC REPORTER. THERE BEING NO OBJECTION, THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY BOTH SIDES. COURT AND COUNSEL DISCUSS THE LODGING OF AN ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD. THE PETITION OF MANDATE IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR IN LIGHT OF THE COURT'S RULING ON THE REQUEST TO AMEND PETITION. COUNSEL FOR THE CITY IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER MARGARET MURRAY'S REQUEST TO AMEND PETITION OF MANDATE. THE COURT MAKES DISCLOSURES REGARDING PAST PROFESSIONAL RELATIONSHIP WITH BOTH COUNSEL PRESENT AS REFLECTED IN THE TRANSCRIPT OF THE CERTIFIEC REPORTER. THERE BEING NO OBJECTION, THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY BOTH SIDES. COURT AND COUNSEL DISCUSS THE LODGING OF AN ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD. THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED, SUBJECT TO THE PARTIES MEETING AND CONFERRING IN ADVANCE OF THE FILING OF THE AMENDED PETITION AND TO TRYING IN GOOD FAITH TO RESOLVE ANY ISSUES REGARDING THE AMENDED PETITION. PETITIONER IS GRANTED LEAVE OF 20 DAYS, OR UNTIL THE CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON AUGUST 31, 2016 TO FILE AND AMENDED PETITION. THE CITY MUST THEN FILE A FILE "VERIFIED" ANSWER RESPONDING LINE BY LINE. THE PARTIES ARE FURTHER ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING AN ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD. THE PETITIONER IS TO LODGE SAID ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD WITH THE COURT UPON FILING OF THE AMENDED PETITION. COUNSEL FOR THE CITY IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576

calendar.matters:
Petitioner Margaret Murray'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Oct-12-2016 Text Ruling

Notice Hearing On Petition Of Mandate
Off Calendar Oct-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: In re: PETITIONER MARGARET MURRAY Notice Hearing On Petition Of Mandate - see minutes for this date

Request To Amend Petition
Off Calendar Aug-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Michael A. Mendelson, Esq. (415-205-0239) appears as counsel for petitioner, Margaret Murray

Deputy City Attorney, Margaret W. Baumgartner of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (415-554-3859) appears as counsel for respondent, San Francisco Board of Supervisors

The Court makes disclosures regarding past professional relationship with both counsel present as reflected in the transcript of the certifiec reporter. There being no objection, the cause is argued and submitted by both sides. Court and counsel discuss the lodging of an administrative record.

PETITIONER MARGARET MURRAY'S Request To Amend Petition of Mandate is GRANTED, subject to the parties meeting and conferring in advance of the filing of the amended petition and to trying in good faith to resolve any issues regarding the amended petition. Petitioner is granted leave of 20 days, or until the close of business on August 31, 2016 to file and Amended Petition. The City must then file a file "verified" answer responding line by line. The parties are further ordered to meet and confer regarding an administrative record. The petitioner is to lodge said administrative record with the Court upon filing of the amended petition. Counsel for the City is directed to submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

Ntc Of Hearing On Petition Of Mandate
Off Calendar Aug-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Michael A. Mendelson, Esq. (415-205-0239) appears as counsel for petitioner, Margaret Murray

Deputy City Attorney, Margaret W. Baumgartner of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (415-554-3859) appears as counsel for respondent, San Francisco Board of Supervisors

The Court makes disclosures regarding past professional relationship with both counsel present as reflected in the transcript of the certifiec reporter. There being no objection, the cause is argued and submitted by both sides. Court and counsel discuss the lodging of an administrative record.

PETITIONER MARGARET MURRAY'S Petition Of Mandate is ordered OFF CALENDAR in light of the Court's ruling on the Request to Amend Petition. Counsel for the City is directed to submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

Motion To Set Hearing For The Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jun-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
